title: BAI Prez Himanta Biswa Sarma on Monkeys at India Open
indian_angle: The head of Indian badminton makes a unique statement regarding an incident at a major tournament in India.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma humorously addressed an incident where monkeys were present during the India Open, questioning the issue if they came to watch the game.

title: Dates Changed for Sub-Junior National Ranking Tournaments
indian_angle: This announcement directly impacts the schedule and participation of many young Indian badminton players across the country.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced changes to the dates of the Yonex-Sunrise All India Sub-Junior National Ranking Badminton Tournaments in Jaipur (U-15 & U-17) and Patna (U-13).

title: Indian Shuttlers Should Learn to Think on Their Feet
indian_angle: This article discusses the crucial aspect of Indian players developing self-reliance and strategic thinking on court.
summary: An opinion piece emphasizes the need for Indian badminton players to cultivate independent decision-making skills during matches, reducing their dependence on coaches.

title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring for All Domestic Tournaments to Debut
indian_angle: This new scoring system will significantly impact junior categories in Indian domestic badminton, potentially shaping future talent.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, set to debut at Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Falters at Last Step in US Open Final
indian_angle: India's Kidambi Srikanth reached a significant final at the US Open, showcasing his continued competitive form on the international stage.
summary: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open came to an end in the final, where he was unable to clinch the title.

title: Shuttler Rounak Chouhan Defeats World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian talent Rounak Chouhan secured a major upset victory over a top-ranked player at an international event.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a stir at the US Open by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen.
